headline: The deadly cheetah trafficking between Somaliland and the Gulf
description: The feline is highly sought after by wealthy collectors of wild animals, who are numerous on the Arabian Peninsula. Across the way, in the Horn of Africa, Somaliland has become the main hub of a thriving trafficking network, even as the species itself is under threat.

headline: Israeli army says 'struck senior Hezbollah commander' in Beirut
Trump says US to leave Iran 'very soon,' deal or not: 'We're finishing the job'
'In their conflict with Iran, the US and Israel are pursuing divergent war aims'
description: While Benjamin Netanyahu seeks to topple a regime that the vast majority of Israelis view as an existential threat, Donald Trump is driven by absolute mercantilism, argues geopolitical expert Frédéric Encel.

headline: French archives of Spaniards who fled Franco's dictatorship are now available online
description: From March 30, over 180,000 records are accessible on the French Office for the Protection of Refugees and Stateless Persons' website, due to funding from Spain aimed at preserving the memory of people's 40 years of exile in France.

headline: Robert Capa, a photographer in the heat of the action, at Paris's Liberation Museum
description: A compelling exhibition, which features a montage of archival footage showing the work of Robert Capa, the Hungarian cofounder of Magnum Photos, details his extraordinary life.

headline: On housing, Mayor Grégoire wants people to 'still dream of living' in Paris
description: The new mayor has pledged to create 30,000 new social housing units and 30,000 additional 'affordable' homes – an ambitious goal. To reach it, Paris will have to nearly double its annual production of public housing.

headline: France's far right splits over the question of sovereignty as several town halls remove EU flags

Haiti gang attacks kill at least 70 people over the weekend, says UN

Céline Dion picks Paris for her return to the stage after a six-year hiatus
Joël Soudron, one of France's most notorious drug traffickers, is arrested in Panama
The French cadets suspected of setting a boat ablaze in Japan
description: An investigation is underway into how four cadets from France's elite Saint-Cyr military academy came to be accused of setting a boat on fire while on leave in Okinawa in 2024. The incident has fueled local distrust of military personnel on the island.

headline: Spice up your life! Five Paris restaurants where you can eat spicy food
description: Mexican chipotle, Korean kimchi, Tunisian harissa: Whether mild or fiery, these condiments add zest to dishes from around the world. Here are five addresses in Paris and Montreuil to awaken your taste buds.

headline: Volodymyr Zelensky's exclusive interview with Le Monde on the war in Ukraine
description: In an interview with Le Monde, the Ukrainian president shares his perspective on a possible end to the conflict and urges the US and all allied nations to maintain their support for Ukraine.

headline: France has yet to find a forward-looking political discourse on racism
'Trump would benefit from rereading Obama's 2015 speech on Iran'
Iranians are caught in the trap of Trump's incoherence
